description:
## Background

Issue Location links can target another remembered Workspace, an unknown Workspace, a subdirectory, or a symlink. Comparing raw strings is incorrect: the existing Workspace flow defines identity as the validated canonical Git root. Beadsmith must discover that identity without mutating the Workspace Catalog, then ask before switching.

Triage: `ready-for-agent`.

## What to build

Open deep links across Workspaces with explicit confirmation and atomic failure behavior. Resolve the candidate path to its canonical Workspace without side effects, skip confirmation when it resolves to Current, and use the normal Workspace-selection transaction only after acceptance.

## Technical Approach

- Add a typed read-only Workspace-candidate resolution operation at the existing backend RPC boundary. It validates Beadwork membership and resolves the canonical Git root but does not load Issues, change Current Workspace, alter MRU, or mutate the Workspace Catalog.
- Reuse the existing Workspace validation implementation rather than duplicating Git-root and Beadwork rules.
- Compare the resolved canonical root with Current Workspace identity.
- If it resolves to Current, open the Issue directly without confirmation, including subdirectory and symlink aliases.
- If it differs, show an accessible confirmation dialog naming the target path and explaining whether the Workspace is already remembered or will be added.
- With no Current Workspace, ask whether to add the Workspace and open the Issue.
- On acceptance, call the normal atomic Workspace-selection operation. Navigate only after the matching Workspace and Issue Explorer snapshot commits.
- On decline, discard the request with no route, selection, Current Workspace, or catalog changes.
- If a newer valid deep link arrives while confirmation is open, replace the pending request and update the dialog. Do not queue prompts.
- If candidate resolution or accepted switching fails, preserve the current route, Workspace, and Issue Explorer snapshot and show the global error banner.
- After a successful switch, reveal the target through All Issues/cleared search as necessary or render Issue-not-found in Issue Detail.

## Considerations

- Read-only resolution must not accidentally call catalog retention or Current Workspace publication.
- Validation can be slow; a newer link must supersede a stale resolution result just as newer Workspace selections supersede stale switch work.
- The existing switch validates again after confirmation. Duplicate validation is acceptable if it preserves one authoritative mutation path and guards against filesystem changes between resolution and acceptance.
- An unknown but valid Workspace enters the catalog only through accepted normal selection. Decline and validation failure leave no retained entry.
- A Workspace may become unavailable after the dialog appears; accepted switch failure follows normal atomic preservation behavior.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] A typed read-only operation resolves candidate directories, subdirectories, and symlinks to canonical Workspace roots without mutating backend state.
- [ ] A canonical alias of Current Workspace opens without confirmation.
- [ ] A different remembered Workspace requires confirmation before switching.
- [ ] An unknown valid Workspace or no-Current-Workspace case clearly asks to add and open it.
- [ ] Acceptance uses the normal Workspace transaction and adds an unknown Workspace only after confirmation.
- [ ] Decline leaves catalog, Current Workspace, route, search, view, and Issue selection unchanged.
- [ ] Newer valid links replace pending resolution/confirmation state.
- [ ] Resolution and switch failures preserve the current atomic snapshot and show the global error banner.
- [ ] Successful switches open hidden targets or render Issue-not-found as agreed.
- [ ] Workspace identity and Issue Explorer snapshot never visibly mismatch.

## Test Plan

- Add backend tests proving read-only canonical resolution and zero state/store mutation on success and failure.
- Add renderer integration tests for current aliases, remembered/unknown/no-current prompts, accept, decline, latest-link-wins, stale async completion, switch failure, hidden target, and missing target.
- Extend real-desktop acceptance with two disposable Workspaces sharing an Issue ID, proving confirmation and correct target data.
- Run Rust tests, renderer tests, transition-gate tests, TypeScript build, Ultracite, Tauri build, and available desktop acceptance tests.
title: Confirm and open cross-Workspace deep links
